popularStory.headline: TRUE HISTORY TO CLEAR UP everyone’s Mess.  Laura Parkes
popularStory.description:
In 1390 Archibald Douglas married Lady Margaret Stewart (d.1451), eldest daughter of John Stewart, Earl of Carrick, who later became King Robert III. 
Of their children:

Archibald Douglas, 5th Earl o …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Elizabeth Douglas於大約1394年出生在英國蘇格蘭藍納可爾道格拉斯時，她的父親Archibald Douglas 4th Earl of Douglas26歲，母親Lady Margaret Stewart25歲。她於1411年5月1日與John Stewart , Earl of Buchan結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。她去世於1451年在英國蘇格蘭斯可迪夏 波德斯特拉華爾享年58歲，埋葬在英國蘇格蘭斯可迪夏 波德斯特拉華爾。

nameMeanings.name: Douglas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from any of various places called from their situation on a river named with Gaelic dubh ‘dark, black’ + glas ‘stream’ (a derivative of glas ‘blue’). There are several localities in Scotland and Ireland so named, but the one from which the surname is derived in most if not all cases is Douglas in Lanarkshire 20 miles south of Glasgow, the original stronghold of the influential Douglas family and their retainers.

History: The family taking their name from Douglas in Lanarkshire were of Flemish origin. They rose to great prominence in the 14th and 15th centuries, controlling the earldoms of Douglas, Morton, and Angus, and later, Queensberry.

variants.name: Douglass
meanings: Scottish and English (Durham and Northumbria): variant of   Douglas  .

History: William Douglass, a physician recognized for his identification and description of an epidemic of scarlet fever, was born c. 1691 in Gifford, Haddington County, Scotland, and settled in Boston in 1718. The abolitionist, orator, and journalist Frederick Douglass assumed the name after escaping from slavery in 1838 and traveling to Massachusetts. Son of a white father and a slave with some Indian blood, he was born Frederick Augustus Washington Bailey c. 1817 in Tuckahoe, MD.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Elizabeth Douglas was born about 1394, in Douglas, Lanarkshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, her father, Archibald Douglas 4th Earl of Douglas, was 26 and her mother, Lady Margaret Stewart, was 25. She married John Stewart , Earl of Buchan on 1 May 1411. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. She died in 1451, in Traquair, Scottish Borders, Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 58, and was buried in Traquair, Scottish Borders, Scotland, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Elizabeth Douglas est née environ 1394, à Douglas, Lanarkshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, son père, Archibald Douglas 4th Earl of Douglas, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Lady Margaret Stewart, avait 25 ans. Elle a épousé John Stewart , Earl of Buchan le 1 mai 1411. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1451, à Traquair, Scottish Borders, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 58 ans, et a été enterrée à Traquair, Scottish Borders, Écosse, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth Douglasが約1394年にイギリススコットランドラナルクシュアイアドウグラスで生まれた時，父Archibald Douglas 4th Earl of Douglasは26歳，母Lady Margaret Stewartは25歳であった。1411年5月1日にJohn Stewart , Earl of Buchanと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1451年にイギリススコットランドスコッティッシュ ボルダーストラクアで58歳で死去し，イギリススコットランドスコッティッシュ ボルダーストラクアにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth Douglas(이)가 대략 1394년 영국 스코틀랜드 랜아르크실르 더글라스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Archibald Douglas 4th Earl of Douglas(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Margaret Stewart(은)는 25세였다. 그녀는 1411년 5월 1일 John Stewart , Earl of Buchan(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1451년 영국 스코틀랜드 스콧이시 볼더스 트라퀄어에서 58세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스코틀랜드 스콧이시 볼더스 트라퀄어에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Elizabeth Douglas родилась около 1394 г. в местности доуглас, ланаркшир, Шотландия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Archibald Douglas 4th Earl of Douglas было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Margaret Stewart, 25. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Stewart , Earl of Buchan 1 мая 1411 г. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1451 г. в местности тракуеир, скоттиш бордирс, Шотландия, Великобритания в возрасте 58 лет и была похоронена в местности тракуеир, скоттиш бордирс, Шотландия, Великобритания.
